items.caption:
On today’s menu 🥦🤍 little homemade goodness made with the Grownsy￼ Baby Food Maker ✨ definitely making it easier to prep fresh meals at home 🫶

Go amazon search for Grownsy Baby Food Maker 🤍
Discount code: HOMEWITHC75

#grownsybabyfoodmaker #firstfoods #babyrecipes #babyledweaning #startingsolids 6months

@grownsy
items.transcript: Make a very simple broccoli and pesto pasta. I've been using the pasta stars from Sainsbury's. They are absolutely perfect. What you need is broccoli, pesto and some soft cheese. Of course, I am using my Groundzy baby food maker. This has honestly been an absolute lifesaver for my baby-led weaning journey. With all these different settings, I've just put my broccoli in to steam away. While that is steaming, I've put my pasta on. I've got my broccoli and I can get a fork through it. So I drain that out. I'm just putting that into the main compartment so I can blend it all up. Then add the soft cheese and then also the pesto. If you'd like, you can add cheese at this stage too. Just blending that all up to make a little sauce. And now my pasta is done. I'm going to offer Ophelia in two different ways. Chunky and smooth. Just mixing the pasta and the sauce together. And I'm also going to add the pasta to the baby food maker and blend that up. Plating it up, chunky and smooth. Adding some broccoli and even a banana as well. I find that Ophelia is preferring smooth food over chunky. I just wanted to give her the two options for this. Oh my goodness, she absolutely loved it. It was so cute eating the broccoli. I was very surprised. She actually really liked the chunky version of it as well. What I love about this is that there is plenty of leftovers so that can either go in the fridge for tomorrow or even the freezer. I just love that I've got that baby food maker. I can steam, I can blend, I can do so many different things in it. This is what was left over from Heart Eating or Lunch.
